title: Pan Crisped Argentinian Potato Empanadas
description: A crispy and savory Argentinian dish featuring potatoes and a smoky flavor, perfect for a midnight craving. This recipe is shojin-friendly and can be served on a tagliere.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the diced potatoes, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook the potato mixture for 10-12 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and lightly browned.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon the potato mixture onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Heat a large skillet with about 1/2 inch of oil over medium heat and fry the empanadas until they are crispy and golden brown.
Drain the empanadas on paper towels and serve hot.
